title: Health & Safety Administrator
description:
Job Opportunity: Health & Safety Coordinator

Location: Sheffield Area - S8 Contract Type: Temporary (upto 6 months)Working Pattern: Full Time (39.5 hours/week)Pay Rate: £15.00 - £17.00 per hour (dependent on experience)Hours:
- Monday to Thursday: 9:00am - 5:00pm
- Friday: 9:00am - 4:30pm

The OpportunityAre you a passionate and proactive Health & Safety professional ready to make a difference in an engineering and manufacturing environment? Our client is on the lookout for an experienced Health & Safety Coordinator to support the ongoing development and management of their Health & Safety practises. This is your chance to shine in a hands-on role where you can drive positive change and work closely with colleagues across all levels of the organisation.

As a vital member of the team, you will support the current Health & Safety lead in reviewing and strengthening systems, processes, and compliance standards.

Key Responsibilities
- Enhance and manage Health & Safety practises across the business.
- Conduct, review, and update risk assessments, ensuring compliance and practicality.
- Identify non-compliance areas and recommend corrective actions.
- Assist in implementing new Health & Safety initiatives and improvements.
- Foster a positive safety culture throughout the organisation.
- Collaborate with managers and employees to engage them in best practises.
- Monitor Health & Safety procedures and ensure adherence to company policies.
- Provide guidance and support on Health & Safety matters.
- Maintain accurate records and documentation.

About YouWe are seeking someone who is confident, practical, and ready to take action! You will ideally have:
- NEBOSH certification (essential).
- Previous experience in a Health & Safety role within an industrial, manufacturing, or engineering environment.
- Strong knowledge of risk assessments with confidence in implementing effective controls.
- A knack for identifying issues and driving positive change.
- Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
- A collaborative approach, capable of influencing colleagues at all levels.
- A proactive and positive "can-do" attitude.
- A hands-on approach, with the confidence to challenge when necessary while maintaining strong relationships.

What's on Offer
- Competitive hourly rate of £15.00 - £17.00 per hour.
- A varied and engaging role with a broad range of Health & Safety responsibilities.
- The opportunity to make a genuine impact within the organisation.
- Potential for longer-term opportunities, subject to business requirements and performance.

If you are a motivated Health & Safety professional who thrives on driving improvements and enjoys being part of a collaborative team, we want to hear from you! Apply today and take the first step toward making a meaningful impact in a vibrant environment.

Adecco acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and an employment business for the supply of temporary workers. The Adecco Group UK & Ireland is an Equal Opportunities Employer.

By applying for this role your details will be submitted to Adecco. Our Candidate Privacy Information Statement explaining how we will use your information is available on our website.

title: Property Administrator
description:
Property Administrator

Sheffield Permanent 9-5.30 Monday - Friday - some flexibility Fridays from home

Join a Leading Property Management Team

Are you an organised and detail-oriented administrator looking to build your career within commercial property? Elevation Recruitment Group are seeking a proactive Property Administrator to support a busy Property Management team, helping to ensure the smooth management of a diverse property portfolio.

This is an excellent opportunity to join a collaborative and professional environment where you'll gain exposure to all aspects of commercial property management while developing valuable industry knowledge and skills.

The Role

Working closely with Property Management Surveyors, you'll play a key role in maintaining accurate property records, coordinating reporting, managing property-related costs, and supporting a range of landlord and tenant matters.

Key Responsibilities as a Property Administrator
- Prepare and coordinate management reports, tenancy schedules, and portfolio information
- Maintain accurate property management systems and records
- Manage utility, business rates, insurance, and other property-related queries
- Support service charge budgets, reconciliations, and insurance renewals
- Assist with arrears management, transaction reporting, and payment tracking
- Raise and manage purchase orders
- Coordinate EPC requirements, floor area records, and business rates mitigation
- Support finance and credit control teams with billing and payment processes
- Manage turnover data collection, calculations, and invoicing
- Provide administrative support across the property management team

About You

We're looking for someone who is organised, numerate, and able to manage multiple priorities while maintaining excellent attention to detail.

You will ideally have:
- Experience within commercial property management or a similar administrative role
- Knowledge of property management systems and databases
- An understanding of leases, business rates, utilities, or property-related costs
- Strong communication and organisational skills
- Good IT skills, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
- A proactive approach and willingness to suggest improvements
- Strong attention to detail and accuracy

Why Apply?
- Opportunity to work within a respected commercial property environment
- Varied role with exposure to multiple aspects of property management
- Supportive and collaborative team culture
- Career development opportunities within a growing sector
- Hybrid working and excellent employee benefits

If you're looking for a varied administrative role where you can develop your property management expertise and make a real contribution to a successful team, I'd love to hear from you.
working_arrangement_evidence: You will ideally have: - Experience within commercial property management or a similar administrative role - Knowledge of property management systems and databases - An understanding of leases, business rates, utilities, or property-related costs - Strong communication and organisational skills - Good IT skills, including Excel, Word, and Power Point - A proactive approach and willingness to suggest improvements - Strong attention to detail and accuracy Why Apply? - Opportunity to work within a respected commercial property environment - Varied role with exposure to multiple aspects of property management - Supportive and collaborative team culture - Career development opportunities within a growing sector - Hybrid working and excellent employee benefits If you're looking for a varied administrative role where you can develop your property management expertise and make a real contribution to a successful team, I'd love to hear from you.

title: Procurement Administrator
description:
Morgan Hunt are currently working with a Further Education College based in South Yorkshire who are seeking a Procurement Administrator to join their team on an initial 3-month temporary contract with the chance to turn into a permanent position. As Procurement Administrator, you will act as the first point of contact for procurement enquiries, providing high-quality administrative support to the Procurement function. You will play a key role in ensuring the College's procurement activities are compliant, efficient, and delivered in accordance with financial regulations, procurement policies, and relevant legislation. Your responsibilities will include supporting supplier management, requisition processing, tendering activities, contract administration, and procurement reporting.Key ResponsibilitiesYour duties will include, but are not limited to:
- Act as the first point of contact for procurement enquiries, resolving issues where possible and directing queries to the appropriate colleagues.
- Review purchase requisitions before release to suppliers, ensuring:
- Approved suppliers are used where appropriate.
- Quantities, pricing, and coding are accurate.
- Requisition descriptions are clear and professional.
- Financial Regulations and Procurement Procedures have been followed.
- Support College staff in obtaining quotations and sourcing suitable suppliers.
- Assist with supplier due diligence and on boarding processes.
- Create, maintain, and regularly review supplier records to ensure information remains accurate and up to date.
- Maintain procurement records, documentation, and electronic filing systems.
- Monitor outstanding requisitions and proactively support the timely resolution of delays.
- Investigate and respond to procurement-related enquiries from staff and suppliers.
- Liaise with Accounts Payable to resolve invoice queries, purchase order issues, and discrepancies.
- Manage the Procurement inbox, ensuring correspondence is dealt with promptly and accurately.
- Provide administrative support for tendering exercises and contract management activities.
- Assist with Modern Slavery compliance, including supplier questionnaires and maintaining supporting records.
- Administer the College's corporate credit card process, maintaining records, monitoring expenditure returns and issuing monthly statements.
- Support procurement reporting, data analysis, and performance monitoring.
- Attend meetings as required to support procurement activities and represent the Procurement function.
- Maintain an up-to-date knowledge of college policies, financial regulations, and procurement legislation.
- Work collaboratively with colleagues across Finance, Estates, and other departments to promote compliance with procurement procedures.

These responsibilities may be reviewed and amended to reflect the evolving needs of the College. You may also be required to undertake other duties appropriate to the grade of the post.Person Specification

Morgan Hunt is a multi-award-winning recruitment Business for interim, contract and temporary recruitment and acts as an Employment Agency in relation to temporary vacancies. Morgan Hunt is an equal opportunities employer, job suitability are assessed on merit in accordance with their skills, qualifications and abilities to perform the relevant duties required in a particular role.

description:

Contract type: Temporary Ad HocStart Date: 3rd July - Additional Dates TBCHours: 8:30am-5pm with 1 hour unpaid breakLocation: S9, SheffieldPay Rate: £12.71 per hour

What You'll Do:

- Assisting with administrative tasks as needed
- Maintaining a tidy and professional reception area

What We're Looking For:
- Exceptional communication skills, both verbal and written
- A cheerful and professional attitude
- Strong organisational abilities and attention to detail
- A proactive approach to problem-solving
- Previous experience in a receptionist or customer service role is preferred but not essential

Apply now with your most up to date CV!

title: Senior Business Support Officer - Travel Assessment Team - Local Authority
description:
Senior Business Support Officer
- Hourly Rate: £13.69 PAYE
- Location: Sheffield Centre, Staniforth
- Job Type: Full-time, potential for extension or permanent role

Join our Client in their Travel Assessment Team as a Senior Business Support Officer, where you will handle a variety of responsibilities across three core service areas: SEN Travel Applications, Personal Travel Budgets, and Post-16 Transport Contributions. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and can manage high volumes of work, especially between April and October.

Day-to-day of the role:

SEN Travel Applications (Home-to-School Transport)
- Triage new applications using a checklist to ensure completeness, correct mileage, and all required information.
- Prepare cases for the weekly Travel Panel, assist panel members by sharing screens, navigating systems, and helping determine application criteria.
- Process outcomes from the panel, including issuing acceptance or rejection letters, selecting appropriate reason codes, and sending letters to parents.

Personal Travel Budgets (PTB)
- Assess applications for PTB, decide on approval or rejection, calculate mileage and payment amounts.
- Send offers, letters, and follow-up communications via Power Apps.
- Maintain and update spreadsheets, chase missing information, and track responses.

Post-16 Transport Contributions
- Manage records on spreadsheets and the Integra system, handle termly invoicing, and chase outstanding payments.
- Ensure invoice details are correct and manage financial records accurately.

General Duties Across All Areas
- Handle high call volumes, including difficult or emotional conversations with parents.
- Maintain GDPR and data protection compliance.
- Work accurately with financial/administrative systems such as Integra.
- Manage workload effectively in a high-pressure, time-sensitive environment.

Required Skills & Qualifications
- Excellent communication skills and strong attention to detail.
- Confidence in using Excel and experience with financial or administrative systems (Integra or similar).
- Ability to handle challenging or emotional conversations.
- Good time-management skills and ability to handle high workloads.
- Understanding of GDPR and data protection.

Benefits
- Competitive hourly rate.
- Travel expenses covered for work-related travel.
- Flexible working hours and hybrid working options after training.
- Opportunity for role extension or permanency.

To apply for the Senior Business Support Officer position, please submit your CV and cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role.
working_arrangement_evidence: Benefits - Competitive hourly rate. - Travel expenses covered for work-related travel. - Flexible working hours and hybrid working options after training. - Opportunity for role extension or permanency.

title: Sales Administrator
description:
Join a growing and dynamic organisation in Sheffield as a Sales Administrator, where your organisation, customer focus, and drive will play a key role in supporting a high-performing sales function. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated Sales Administrator to join a friendly team, develop their skills, and contribute to continued business success. If you're looking for a varied and rewarding role with real progression opportunities, this Sales Administrator position could be the ideal next step in your career.

Key Duties
- Support internal and external sales teams in achieving targets through efficient administrative and customer support
- Manage quotes, orders, and sales administration tasks with high accuracy and attention to detail
- Build and maintain strong relationships with customers, suppliers, and internal teams
- Follow up on leads, assist with new business generation, and handle customer enquiries via phone and email

Person Specification
- Strong IT skills, including Outlook, Excel, and Word
- Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
- Highly organised with strong attention to detail
- Proactive, adaptable, and able to work both independently and as part of a team

Benefits
- Free parking and on-site parking facilities
- Company pension scheme
- Health and wellbeing programme
- Supportive working environment with full training and development opportunities

Apply ASAP as interviews will be taking place this week

title: Import / Export Administrator
description:
Are you an organised and detail-driven professional looking to develop your career in international logistics? We are seeking an Import / Export Co-Ordinator to join a growing Sheffield-based team supporting global operations. This is an excellent opportunity for an Import / Export Co-Ordinator to build hands-on experience in customs compliance, freight coordination, and supply chain processes within a collaborative and supportive environment. For the right candidate, upskilling can be provided to train on areas where there may be a lack of experience.

Key Duties
- Monitor and review HMRC MSS Export Reports, ensuring all export activity is fully compliant
- Liaise with freight forwarders to obtain proof of export and export declarations across multiple entities
- Collate and maintain accurate export documentation to support HMRC compliance requirements
- Manage shipping invoices and provide support to purchasing teams with import clearance instructions when required

Person Specification
- Previous experience in a logistics, customs, or supply chain role (6 months+ preferred) or logistics based educational background
- Knowledge of import/export regulations and freight coordination processes
- Proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel, with excellent organisational skills
- Confident communicator with the ability to build effective working relationships both internally and externally

Benefits
- Competitive salary with opportunities for progression within a global organisation
- Comprehensive training and development to enhance your logistics and compliance expertise
- Supportive team environment with cross-functional collaboration
- Company pension scheme and generous holiday allowance

If you're ready to take the next step in your logistics career as an Import / Export Co-Ordinator, apply today and become part of a team committed to excellence in international trade. Interviews will take place ASAP with an ASAP start date

title: Receptionist / Facilities Coordinator
description:
Catch 22 are recruiting on behalf of a prestigious private organisation in Barnsley for a professional and proactive who urgently require a receptionist with knowledge of facilities management.

This is a varied front-of-house role, combining reception duties with facilities coordination responsibilities. You will be the first point of contact for visitors, manage meeting room bookings, support office operations, coordinate contractors and suppliers, and help ensure the workplace runs smoothly and efficiently.

Key Responsibilities:
- Deliver a professional front-of-house reception service
- Meet and greet visitors and manage incoming calls
- Coordinate meeting rooms and office facilities
- Liaise with contractors, suppliers and service providers
- Support health & safety and workplace compliance activities
- Assist with general office administration and facilities tasks

About You:
- Previous reception, facilities or office coordination experience
- Excellent communication and customer service skills
- Highly organised with strong attention to detail
- Proficient in Microsoft Office applications
- Professional, approachable and able to work independently
- Able to start immediately

This is an excellent opportunity to join a well-established private organisation offering a supportive working environment and long-term career prospects.

title: Customer Service Admin
description:
Customer Service Administrator - 6 month FTC (Sept - March)

Location: RotherhamContract: 6-Month Fixed-Term ContractSalary: £26 - 28k per annum

Elevation Business Support are delighted to be recruiting for a Customer Service Administrator on behalf of a growing and fast-paced business. This is a varied and hands-on role that would suit someone who enjoys a mix of administration, customer service, order processing and operational support.

Working within a small and collaborative team, you'll need to be an adaptable all-rounder who is happy to support wherever needed. No two days are quite the same, and whilst some aspects of the role can be repetitive, the variety of responsibilities will keep you engaged and challenged.

Key Responsibilities

Order Processing & Administration
- Processing customer orders received through the company website and third-party online platforms.
- Using internal systems to review orders and determine whether shipments can progress automatically or require further action.
- Verifying order information, shipping details, product selections and courier labels before dispatch.
- Generating shipping labels and ensuring all documentation is accurate.
- Uploading tracking information to customer orders and keeping records updated.

Warehouse & Dispatch Support
- During quieter periods, assisting with the picking and preparation of customer orders.
- Checking products against picking lists to ensure accuracy before dispatch.
- Supporting the wider team with general operational tasks to help maintain service levels.

Customer Service
- Responding to customer enquiries via telephone and email.
- Investigating and resolving issues relating to damaged items, delivery queries and orders not received.
- Providing a professional and empathetic service when handling customer concerns.
- Raising tickets and liaising directly with courier providers to investigate delivery issues.

Courier & Logistics Management
- Proactively monitoring shipments and courier performance.
- Chasing courier partners where delivery updates are required.
- Ensuring customers receive timely and accurate information regarding their orders.

About You

We're looking for someone who enjoys being busy and can confidently manage a variety of tasks throughout the day.

The successful candidate will have:
- Previous experience within customer service, administration, order processing or a similar role.
- Strong communication skills with the confidence to handle customer queries by phone and email.
- Excellent attention to detail and accuracy.
- Good IT skills and the ability to learn internal systems quickly.
- A flexible and team-focused approach.
- The willingness to support with light warehouse and picking duties when required.
- Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to remain calm under pressure.

Why Apply?
- Varied role combining customer service, administration and operational support.
- Opportunity to work within a friendly, supportive and collaborative team.
- Exposure to multiple areas of the business.
- Ideal for someone who enjoys a hands-on position and being part of a small business environment where everyone helps each other.
- Available as either a 6-month fixed-term contract or temporary assignment with week-by-week extensions.

If you're looking for a role where you can utilise your customer service skills while also getting involved with the wider operation of a business, we'd love to hear from you.

For more information or to apply, please contact Elevation Business Support today.

title: Loan Administrator
description:
Job Title: Loan Administrator

Location: Rotherham

Salary: Up to £25,000 DOE OTE £30,000

Hours: 37.5 hour week.Monday 9 am to 6 pm, Tuesday to Thursday 9 am to 5.30 pm, Friday 9 am to 5 pm

Benefits:
- 22 days' holiday + bank holidays (+5 with service)
- Sick pay, income protection & life assurance
- EAP, virtual GP & wellbeing support
- Pension, Cycle to Work & discounts
- Holiday buy/sell scheme
- Development & supportive culture

About the position of Loan Administrator:

An exciting opportunity to join a fast-paced, supportive team, providing key administrative support to our Loans and Business Development teams. You'll ensure applications progress efficiently and deliver excellent service to customers and brokers throughout their journey.

Responsibilities for the role of Loan Administrator:
- Issue ESIS documents in a timely and compliant manner
- Contact customers and support advisor overflow work, progressing applications where appropriate
- Arrange property valuations and request mortgage references
- Process and pay invoices
- Produce daily and weekly management reports
- Transfer hot keys and follow up with customers
- Prepare product reports for the Business Development team
- Maintain accurate customer and broker records across systems
- Provide general administrative support to Loans and Development teams
- Ensure all work is completed accurately, efficiently, and in line with procedures and service standards

Experience and skills required for the role of Loan Administrator:
- Highly organised with strong administrative skills
- Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy
- Hard-working, proactive, and able to manage a varied and fast-paced workload
- Strong written and verbal communication skills
- A collaborative team player, supporting colleagues to achieve shared goals
- Confident working in a fast-paced financial services environment with the ability to prioritise effectively
- Previous experience within financial services is essential, ideally within mortgages or secured lending
- Experience in a mortgage, loan processing, or similar regulated financial services environment is highly desirable

For more information regarding the role of Loans Administrator please contact us

Stellar Select is acting as an employment agency and is a corporate member of the REC.

Due to a high volume of applications, we will only contact you if your application has been successful. We aim to contact all successful contacts within two business days.

title: Planning Administrator
description:
Planning Administrator 3 month temp to permLocation: UK - Rotherham Full Time Onsite £30k

Mon - Thurs: 8-4.30 Fri 1pm finish

Elevation Recruitment Group are seeking a proactive and highly organised Planning Administrator to join a busy manufacturing planning team. This role plays a key part in supporting production scheduling, ensuring manufacturing orders are accurate, and helping to maintain smooth and efficient factory operations. Starting as 3 month temporary position, leading in to a permanent role for the right person.

Working closely with machine shop and shop floor supervisors, you will support the planning function by coordinating weekly manufacturing orders, updating key systems, and contributing to continuous improvement across the department.

Key Responsibilities of a Planning Administrator
- Produce and distribute weekly manufacturing orders in line with production requirements
- Work closely with operations teams to support factory processes and planning needs
- Provide clear direction to shop floor supervisors in line with planning schedules
- Create, update, and maintain manufacturing orders and system data
- Monitor sales progress and chase lists, updating relevant reports
- Develop daily production and wrapping plans to support output targets
- Run and support inventory meetings, contributing to usage and efficiency improvements
- Produce daily and weekly on-time delivery (OTD) and performance reports
- Update KPI dashboards and system reports accurately and on time
- Support production scheduling to maintain factory efficiency and delivery performance
- Carry out general planning administration and housekeeping tasks
- Assist with nesting parts for laminates where required

About You
- Strong organisational and planning skills with high attention to detail
- Confident using Microsoft Excel and other Microsoft Office tools
- Strong numerical and analytical ability
- Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
- Ability to work accurately with complex and high-value production requirements
- Strong team player with the ability to work independently when required
- Quick to learn and confident working with systems and processes

This is a great opportunity to join a fast-paced manufacturing environment where you will play a key role in production planning and operational efficiency. You will gain exposure across multiple departments and contribute directly to improving factory performance and delivery success. Get in touch with Kelly West today.

title: Care Coordinator
description:
Care Coordinator - Full time £27,000 to £32,000 DOE - Doncaster

We are currently recruiting an experienced Care Coordinator to join a well-established domiciliary care provider in Leeds. This is a full-time, permanent role working Monday to Friday, offering a salary of £27,000 to £32,000 per year, depending on experience.

This is a hands-on and fast-paced position that requires flexibility, as you may be required to support colleagues and other departments when needed. Previous experience within a domiciliary care setting is essential.

Benefits include:
- Competitive salary with monthly bonuses
- Mileage allowance
- Full training and ongoing CPD
- Pension contribution
- Supportive and positive working environment

A full UK driving licence and access to your own vehicle are required for this role.

Key Responsibilities:
- Producing and managing staff rotas to ensure all care calls are covered
- Liaising with hospitals, GPs, social services and families to support service users
- Completing care assessments and reviews to maintain high standards of care
- Managing and participating in the on-call rota

If you are looking to join a growing and supportive care provider in a rewarding role, we would love to hear from you.

To apply, please send your CV to Chase Medical.
